ogImageAlt: Amelia Earhart and her Lockheed Electra, 1937

routes.title: Amelia's Flight — The 1937 World Flight
routes.description: An interactive tribute to Amelia Earhart's 1937 around-the-world flight, traced leg by leg on a rotating globe — twenty-nine legs across ocean and desert, and the final dawn she never crossed.
routes.heading: Amelia's Flight: The 1937 World Flight
routes.summary: A scroll-driven retelling of Amelia Earhart's 1937 attempt to circle the globe along the equator, told one leg at a time on a rotating map — from Oakland and Miami, across the Caribbean, Africa, Asia, and the Pacific, to the last leg toward Howland Island.
routes.keywords: Amelia Earhart, 1937 world flight, Lockheed Electra, around the world flight, Fred Noonan, aviation history

routes.title: The Records — Amelia Earhart's Aviation Firsts | Amelia's Flight
routes.description: Amelia Earhart's record-setting flights and aviation firsts — the achievements that made the 1937 world flight the capstone of a record-breaking career.
routes.heading: The Records
routes.summary: Amelia Earhart's aviation records and firsts — from her solo Atlantic crossing to her transcontinental and Pacific records — the milestones that led to the 1937 around-the-world attempt.
routes.keywords: Amelia Earhart records, aviation firsts, solo Atlantic flight, women in aviation

routes.title: The Pilots — Amelia Earhart & Fred Noonan | Amelia's Flight
routes.description: The two aviators aboard the Lockheed Electra: pilot Amelia Earhart and navigator Fred Noonan, the pair who set out to fly around the world in 1937.
routes.heading: The Pilots
routes.summary: Profiles of Amelia Earhart, the record-setting aviator, and Fred Noonan, her expert celestial navigator — the two people aboard the Lockheed Electra on the 1937 world flight.
routes.keywords: Amelia Earhart, Fred Noonan, navigator, Lockheed Electra crew

routes.title: The Plane — Lockheed Model 10-E Electra (NR16020) | Amelia's Flight
routes.description: Amelia Earhart's aircraft: the twin-engine Lockheed Model 10-E Electra, registration NR16020, the 'Flying Laboratory' she flew on the 1937 world flight.
routes.heading: The Plane
routes.summary: The Lockheed Model 10-E Electra (NR16020) — the twin-engine all-metal monoplane, modified as a 'Flying Laboratory' with extra fuel tanks, that carried Amelia Earhart and Fred Noonan on the 1937 around-the-world attempt.
routes.keywords: Lockheed Electra, Model 10-E, NR16020, Flying Laboratory, Amelia Earhart plane

routes.title: The Island — Howland Island, the Destination Never Reached | Amelia's Flight
routes.description: Howland Island: the tiny speck of Pacific coral that was Amelia Earhart's intended landfall on July 2, 1937, and the airstrip she never reached.
routes.heading: The Island
routes.summary: Howland Island — a flat, two-mile sliver of coral in the central Pacific where a crushed-coral airstrip and the cutter Itasca waited for Amelia Earhart on July 2, 1937. She never arrived.
routes.keywords: Howland Island, Itasca, Pacific, Amelia Earhart destination, 1937

routes.title: The Last Hours — Earhart's Final Radio Transmissions | Amelia's Flight
routes.description: The final hours of the 1937 flight reconstructed from the USCGC Itasca's radio log — Amelia Earhart's last messages before the Lockheed Electra vanished.
routes.heading: The Last Hours
routes.summary: A reconstruction of Amelia Earhart's final radio transmissions on July 2, 1937, drawn from the radio log of the Coast Guard cutter Itasca — the fading calls for a position fix in the hours before the Electra disappeared.
routes.keywords: Amelia Earhart last transmission, Itasca radio log, final hours, 1937 disappearance

routes.title: The Archives — Photographs & Documents | Amelia's Flight
routes.description: An archive of photographs, documents, postal covers, and ephemera from Amelia Earhart's 1937 world flight and the search that followed.
routes.heading: The Archives
routes.summary: Archival photographs, documents, flight covers, markings, and stamps connected to Amelia Earhart's 1937 world flight and the subsequent search.
routes.keywords: Amelia Earhart archives, photographs, flight covers, documents, 1937

routes.title: The Theories — What Happened to Amelia Earhart | Amelia's Flight
routes.description: The leading theories of Amelia Earhart's 1937 disappearance — crash-and-sink, the Nikumaroro castaway hypothesis, capture at Saipan, and more — weighed side by side.
routes.heading: The Theories
routes.summary: The competing explanations for Amelia Earhart's disappearance: the official crash-and-sink theory, the TIGHAR Nikumaroro castaway hypothesis, the Marshall Islands and Saipan capture claims, the espionage theory, and the survival legends — each summarized and assessed.
routes.keywords: Amelia Earhart theories, disappearance, Nikumaroro, TIGHAR, crash and sink, Saipan
